summary: chain-sdk-js executes DES-decrypted payload from sibling package on require()
details: Package impersonates the Theta Labs blockchain SDK (real vendor publishes as @thetalabs/theta-js; this unscoped package sets author to 'Theta Labs' with a matching description). The published main bundle dist/chain-sdk-js.cjs.js contains top-level code that reads two encrypted files (rsa.db and des.db) from a sibling runtime dependency 'thedata', DES-decrypts them with a hardcoded password, spawns a node child process, and writes the decrypted plaintext into its stdin — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript on the consumer's machine at the moment of require/import. A duplicate loader ships in dist/thetajs.cjs.js (and umd/esm variants), providing a second import-time RCE vector under a name that also mimics the legitimate theta-js package. Splitting the ciphertext into a separate npm dependency (thedata@^1.0.1) hides the executable payload from scanners that inspect only this package's own tarball. The Theta-SDK cover story targets developers who handle wallet private keys and mnemonics at runtime.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
